Video.summary: Daniel Pelosi was convicted of second-degree murder in the slaying of investment banker and multimillionaire Ted Ammon -- the husband of his lover, Generosa. He is currently serving 25 years to life but now claims he is ready to explain what really happened the night of the murder. Dr. Phil visits Pelosi behind bars.
Video.title: High Society Whodunit

Video.summary: Millions were inspired by the first “Role of the Man” show. Now, Dr. Phil is making it an ongoing series and checking in with past guests. Remember the husband who provided everything for his wife…except intimacy? He’s back! Are they now sleeping in the same bed? Then, you’ll hear from the man who wanted his wife to have sex with him on demand. Did he change his selfish ways or is he still in the doghouse? Plus, what’s a mama’s boy to do when his wife insists he cut the umbilical cord once and for all?
Video.title: Man’s Role (Update)

Video.summary: Tiffany says her relationship with her son’s father, Erik, was destroyed by his constant lying and empty promises, to the point where she completely cut him off and has not seen him in over three years. Erik admits he has lied to his ex-girlfriends. Then, just this past year, Erik dated Sheka who says after doing research midway through their relationship, she uncovered his lies and put a stop to their wedding plans. So, when did Erik begin to con and deceive people? As Erik is accused of one lie more shocking than the next, his mom, Jody, says her son has been lying since he was a child. And, don't miss part 2, when more women come forward from Erik’s past.
Video.title: Our Lying Ex-Boyfriend

Video.summary: A Meddling Mother-in-Law or a Lying Cheating Daughter-in-Law? Dr Phil delves into the family drama.
Video.title: Toxic and Offensive Inlaws

Video.summary: What happens when you can't let go of a meaningful relationship from the past? What are the boundaries when getting in touch with an old flame while you're married? Although toying with old flames can get you burned, it can also be the spark that rekindles lost love.
Video.title: Old Flames

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he can’t pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says he’s slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joey’s mistress, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isn’t Enough

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his conversation with Christina, who emailed him desperate for help for her daughter, Tara, who she says can be an angry monster. Christina left the home when Tara was 13 years old, but now, she says she's worried that her daughter is in a toxic situation with the ex-husband she calls narcissistic and his 22-year-old new wife who is 20 weeks pregnant. Today, meet the new wife and find out if Dr. Phil can help this chaotic triangle.
Video.title: Family Triangle Drama (Part 2)

Video.summary: A woman attempts to confront her cheating husband with his alleged mistresses.
Video.title: Pablo’s Other Women

Video.summary: A divorced couple secretly remarries, but did they make a mistake- again?
Video.title: Make It or Break It

Video.summary: “He loves me. He loves me not.” Wouldn’t it be great if there was a formula to get your partner to commit? Josie has been dating Curry for more than three years, and she says she’s ready to dump him if he doesn’t pop the question soon. Curry says he loves his girlfriend, so why is he afraid to walk down the aisle? Will Josie hold out for a ring, or will she kick this commitment-phobe to the curb? Then, Melissa thought she had it all –- the ring, the dress and the man of her dreams –- until she discovered her wedding was a sham. She says her husband, John, lied about how many times he’d been married, but the biggest tale of all is that he’s still married to another woman! John admits that he lied about being divorced, but says Melissa wouldn’t have stayed with him if he told her the truth. Will Melissa ever be able to trust him again ... and should she? Plus, meet a couple who says their dream wedding turned into a nightmare when the wedding planner scammed them out of $30,000.
Video.title: Commit Or Quit

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues to speak with parents, Liz and Jim, about why each blames the other for their son’s drug addiction. Liz says she blames what she calls Jim’s abusive parenting philosophy for their son retreating to drugs, which Jim vehemently denies. Find out what happens when Jim faces his daughter, Chloe, and son, Nic. Will they support their mother’s accusations against their father?
Video.title: Father Confronts Children (Part 2)

Video.summary: A man's claim of his father's disappearance leads to a shoking decption.
Video.title: Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death

Image.alt: Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death
Dad Accused of Faking His Own Death

Video.summary: Parents Liz and Jim say their 16-year-old son, Nic, has a serious drug problem that has such a hold on him, they claim he even stole $75,000 in gold from his parents’ safe and spent it all on drugs! But, Liz says she is convinced that her husband is to blame. Liz accuses Jim of being a narcissist and misogynist, whose parenting philosophy is based on intimidation and abuse, and claims that is what drove their son to drugs. Jim denies Liz’s allegations, claiming she has gone to great lengths to paint him as a villain. He says she is the major problem, and calls her the world's lousiest parent and a doormat who helped enable Nic's deadly addictions.
Video.title: Teen Drug Problem (Part 1)

Video.summary: Dr. Phil shares advice for dealing with rude, crude and obnoxious behavior from your family members.
Video.title: My Annoying Family

Video.summary: Meleah and Greg's young love story started and ended like so many. But when Meleah discovered she was pregnant following their breakup, their lives became forever intertwined. Watch as sparks fly when they face off! Plus, Paige was a teen mom struggling to make ends meet. You won't want to miss her remarkable update!
Video.title: Baby After Breakup

Video.summary: A mom is supposed to love and nurture her children, but what if she's too involved? Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling, manipulative and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration, not mourning. He claims she uses bribes to control his every move, including offering him $20,000 to divorce his first wife. Margaret admits that she can be pushy and difficult but says she just wants to see her son succeed in life. Is she really setting him up for failure? Does Reed really want to man-up and break free from his mother's grasp? And, is Margaret willing to let him go? Then, 35-year-old Anthony, star of the new TLC reality show Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home and letting his mom, Patti, wait on him hand and foot. How does Patti feel about their living arrangement? Will she ever kick Anthony out? Plus, hear how Anthony's lifestyle affects his dating life. If you know of a mother-son bond that's a little too close for comfort, don't miss this Dr. Phil. Reed, 33, says his mother, Margaret, is so meddling and controlling that the day she dies will be a cause for celebration. Margaret says she wants to see her son succeed - but is she setting him up for failure? Plus, 35-year-old Anthony, star of TLC's Mama's Boys of the Bronx, says he sees nothing wrong with living at home.
Video.title: Over The Top Moms

Video.summary: Parents are concerned about the extreme measures their daughters take to get and keep a boyfriend.
Video.title: Teens Obsessed with Love

Video.summary: Would you rather be right or happy? Dr. Phil talks with some right-fighters who can't settle their debate.
Video.title: I Am Right!

Video.summary: Are we raising a generation of ungrateful children? Melissa’s sisters say her sons are the most spoiled boys in North America. They get everything they want – all they have to do is whine. Melissa says her kids just like toys, and she thinks her sisters may be jealous of her lifestyle. Next, Dori admits that her 13-year-old son, Parker, is spoiled. Parker says he won't take no for an answer, and even has a strategy for getting everything he wants. Find out what's behind Dori's need to spoil Parker. Then, Joan says her 14-year-old daughter, Jacquie, is a snob, and her need for trendy clothes is turning her into a materialistic monster. Plus, Lauren spends her entire paycheck shopping, but with no money in her checking account, she’s afraid she could be headed for trouble. Her mom, Diane, says she's not worried, it's just Lauren's way of relaxing.
Video.title: Spoiled and Entitled

Video.summary: Everyone knows people that love to bicker. But what happens when the bickering escalates into verbal abuse and violence? Dr. Phil checks in with past guests Troy and Tammy whose explosive arguing is the primary dynamic in their marriage. Have they been able to break the cycle of drama or are they one step closer to divorce? Then—meet a single mother of five who worries that her children’s bickering may lead to fatal consequences! She thinks they’re out of control. So why is her daughter pointing the finger at her? Dr. Phil helps her end the fighting once and for all.
Video.title: The Bickersons

Video.summary: Did you know that close to 14 million adults still live at home with their parents? Dr. Phil’s first guest is 30 years old, has 2 kids, and is still living with her mom! Her mom wants her out, so why won’t she leave? Then, meet one mother who made her son sign a contract to move out for good. The deadline has lapsed, but he won’t budge. How far will Dr. Phil go to push him out of the nest? Plus, meet a couple who opened their home to 21 family members over the last 7 years. Finally, their nephew came to stay for a couple of days…and two years later he’s still there!
Video.title: Kick 'Em Out!

Video.summary: Parents: If your teen talks back, acts out or is just plain rude, you better tune in!
Video.title: Rude Teens

Video.summary: The family of a 16-year-old self-professed gang member comes together to urge the teen to turn his life around. And, two former gang members have an important message for the teen -- Is he ready to walk away from the gang life for good?
Video.title: Young, Privileged, And In A Gang

Image.alt: Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang
Young, Privileged, And In A Gang

Video.summary: Amanda Knox spent four years locked in an Italian prison for the murder of her British roommate Meredith Kercher. Dubbed “Foxy Knoxy” by the press, she became one of the most famous cases of wrongful conviction. She tells her story, as she fights to save the life of Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed. This program contains graphic discussion about a criminal case. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Amanda Knox: Freeing The Innocent

Video.summary: Beka is a mom of three teenage girls who she says are angry, violent and out of control. She says an argument with her oldest daughter Arielle escalated to a life-threatening altercation when the 16-year-old stabbed her with a kitchen knife. Arielle was arrested and sent to juvenile detention for the incident, but is she remorseful? And are her 15-year-old identical twin sisters following in her footsteps? Beka says they are "evil" and have threatened to kill her too. But the twins tell a different story, claiming Beka is the one who is manipulative and abusive. They say she drinks excessively, has choked them and has even tried to take her own life. Can Dr. Phil get to the root of the real problem in this tumultuous household? Beka's husband, Jody, weighs in. Plus, Dr. Phil delivers a shocking message to this family in crisis -- Don't miss it.
Video.title: Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen

Image.alt: Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen
Stabbed In The Heart… By My Teen

Video.summary: Bridget says she’s not sure what to believe about the relationship between her 38-year-old brother-in-law, Kevin, and 16-year-old daughter, Grace.Dr. Phil says Bridget is a parent in denial and in need of a wake-up call. He sits down one-on-one with Kevin. How does Kevin explain his actions?
Video.title: Uncle Kevin Responds (Part 2)

Video.summary: "I wanted to make someone jealous." "I was bored." "I wanted to feel closer to God." These are just a few of the 237 reasons why women say they have sex. Author and clinical psychologist Dr. Cindy Meston and Dr. Jennifer Berman, a sexual health expert, join Dr. Phil to talk about women and sex, and offer advice to couples with differing libidos.
Video.title: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Image.alt: The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex
The 237 Reasons Why Women Have Sex

Video.summary: In part two, the estranged husband of missing mom, Julie Ann Gonzalez, takes a polygraph test.
Video.title: Runaway Moms: The Polygraph

Video.summary: Ask any teenager, and he or she will tell you how tough life is: schoolwork, dating and dealing with pushy parents. But imagine having two kids while you're still in high school. Dr. Phil sits down with Corey and Kim, a troubled young couple, to see if their relationship can be saved.
Video.title: Troubled Teen Love

Video.summary: A viral video depicting a judge whipping his daughter sparks outrage.
Video.title: Discipline Video: Judge Accused

Video.summary: Dr. Phil wants a violent teen to meet someone who could change her life. How will she react?
Video.title: Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)

Image.alt: Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)
Destoni’s Drama: Will She Accept Help? (Pt 2)

Video.summary: We've all heard of in-laws who don't get along, but these guests say their problems have become scary and even life-threatening.
Video.title: In-Law Threats!
